title: fix: Patch::Keep silently serializes as null in release builds
description:
## Problem
Patch::Keep's Serialize impl has a debug_assert\!(false) that fires in debug but is silently ignored in release, where Patch::Keep serializes as null — indistinguishable from Patch::Clear on the wire. This will clear server-side nullable fields on any struct where the skip_serializing_if annotation is accidentally omitted.

## Solution
Replace the silent fallback with a hard serde error: Err(serde::ser::Error::custom(...)) so misconfigured fields fail loudly in all build profiles. Alternatively, remove the Serialize impl and require callers to use map_entry() exclusively.

## Acceptance
- Patch::Keep serialization returns an error in release builds (not null)
- Existing usages with skip_serializing_if are unaffected
- Tests verify the error path
